label: Day 3 — Afternoon: Green Habits in Daily Life
description: From footprints to choices: this session uses scenario-based decision-making to explore how everyday habits connect to sustainability outcomes. Participants make choices as characters from different countries and contexts — then examine the consequences.
objectives: Explore the sustainability implications of everyday lifestyle choices
Understand how context (income, infrastructure, culture) shapes choices
Practice systems thinking: tracing choices to upstream and downstream effects
Develop empathy for different lived experiences of sustainability

steps.title: Character assignment
steps.body: Each participant receives a character card: a person from one of the four partner countries with a specific age, income, location, and lifestyle. They will make decisions as that character throughout the session.

steps.title: A Day in the Life — Scenario Choices
steps.body: Using the interactive scenario tool below, participants make 10 everyday choices for their character: morning commute, lunch, shopping, energy use, etc. Each choice has visible sustainability consequences.

steps.title: Debriefing the characters
steps.body: Participants share what surprised them about their character's constraints. Group discussion: which choices were actually choices? Which were determined by context? What does 'green living' mean when you can't afford it?

steps.title: From character to self
steps.body: Return to your own life. Which of your character's constraints do you recognise in yourself? Which privileges do you have that your character didn't? One insight to share.

facilitatorNote: The character exercise only works if participants genuinely inhabit their character rather than playing themselves. The moment of 'I can't choose differently — I can't afford it' is the most powerful in this session. Don't rush past it.
